frozen_components: frozen LMs used as feedback modules
architecture.summary: Multi-Aspect Feedback (MAF) is an iterative refinement framework that routes LM-generated reasoning chains through multiple specialized feedback modules—each a frozen LM or external tool designed to detect and correct a specific error category (hallucination, intermediate reasoning errors, mathematical errors). Corrected reasoning is re-fed to the main LM for refinement across multiple iterations. Information flow: LM output → specialized feedback engines (text-based corrections) → revised input → LM iteration.

biblio.title: MAF: Multi-Aspect Feedback for Improving Reasoning in Large Language Models

claims.statement: improvement of up to 20% in Mathematical Reasoning
improvement of up to 18% in Logical Entailment
existing approaches relying on a single generic feedback source fail to address the diverse error types found in LM-generated reasoning chains
LMs still face challenges such as hallucination, generating incorrect intermediate reasoning steps, and making mathematical errors
Multi-Aspect Feedback integrates multiple feedback modules, including frozen LMs and external tools, each focusing on a specific error category
